title: Indication of sidelink process for sidelink feedback
abstract: This disclosure provides systems, methods and apparatuses for indicating sidelink processes for sidelink feedback. A second user equipment (UE) may request hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) responses from a first UE for a particular set of sidelink processes over a physical sidelink feedback channel. To indicate multiple sidelink processes, the second UE may indicate, in sidelink control information, a window of sidelink processes. The window may be a sliding window that starts with a starting sidelink process and extends through other sidelink processes. The second UE may transmit data for the sidelink processes in the sliding window and receive feedback for the indicated sidelink processes. In some aspects, the first UE may select, in coordination with the second UE, the sidelink processes for which feedback is to be provided.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus of a first user equipment (UE) for wireless communication, comprising: a first interface configured to obtain, from a second UE, sidelink control information (SCI) that indicates a starting sidelink process of a sliding window of sidelink processes for which one or more hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) responses are to be transmitted; and the first interface or a second interface configured to output the one or more HARQ responses to the second UE on a physical sidelink feedback channel (PSFCH). 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the SCI indicates the starting sidelink process with m bits, and wherein m bits is a bit capacity of the PSFCH. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein m is 4. 4. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein a HARQ response is requested for a current sidelink process, and m-1 bits indicate the starting sidelink process for a remainder of the sliding window. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein a HARQ response is not requested for a current sidelink process, wherein the SCI indicates the starting sidelink process with m-1 bits, and wherein m bits is a bit capacity of the PSFCH. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein m is 3. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein m is a specified value stored at the first UE, and wherein the SCI indicates the starting sidelink process with a log2(m)-bit control sequence. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the SCI indicates the sliding window with m bits, and wherein the sliding window is one of multiple candidate sliding windows that are each associated with a bit capacity n of the PSFCH. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein n is 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16, and wherein a quantity of candidate sidelink windows for a value of n is equal to 16 divided by n. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein a quantity of sidelink processes in the sliding window is equal to n. 11 The apparatus of claim 8, wherein n is specified between the first UE and the second UE. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein m is 3 if n is 4, 8, or 16. 13. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein m is 2 if n is 4. 14. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the multiple candidate sliding windows are divided into a first half and a second half, and wherein the indicated sidelink window is from either the first half or the second half based on a current sidelink process. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein m is 3, and wherein the first half and the second half each include 8 sets of sidelink processes. 16. An apparatus of a second user equipment (UE) for wireless communication, comprising: a first interface configured to output, to a first UE, sidelink control information (SCI) that indicates a starting sidelink process of a sliding window of sidelink processes for which one or more hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) responses are to be transmitted; and the first interface or a second interface configured to obtain the one or more HARQ responses from the first UE on a physical sidelink feedback channel (PSFCH). 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the SCI indicates the starting sidelink process with m bits, and wherein m bits is a bit capacity of the PSFCH. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein m is 4. 19. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein m is 3. 22.
